name: Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa
region_intro: Jogging around Vinuesa offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its proximity to the Urbión mountain range and the scenic Laguna Negra. The region features varied terrain, including forest paths, mountain trails, and routes around the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo reservoir. Elevations range from gentle slopes to more challenging ascents, providing options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Vinuesa The most popular jogging route is Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas – trail running circular por el Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route explores the natural park with views of glacial cirques. Local runners also love the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Jogging around Vinuesa is defined by mountain landscapes, reservoir paths, and forest trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various abilities. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Vinuesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Vinuesa?
A: There are over 35 running routes in the Vinuesa area, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy paths to challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Vinuesa?
A: While many routes in Vinuesa are moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there are some easier options. For instance, the area around the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo reservoir offers flatter sections. You can find one easy route and 15 moderate routes, which might be suitable for beginners looking for a bit more challenge.

Q: What are the best seasons for running in Vinuesa?
A: The best seasons for running in Vinuesa are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, though some higher altitude routes might be cooler. Winter running is possible but requires appropriate gear due to snow and colder conditions, particularly in the Urbión mountain range.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in Vinuesa?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Vinuesa are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064184945/embalse-cuerda-del-pozo-running-circular-desde-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop around the reservoir. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa loop from Molinos de Duero&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.6 km route.

Q: Can I find running routes with viewpoints or scenic spots in Vinuesa?
A: Absolutely. The Vinuesa region is known for its stunning natural beauty. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064170909/sierra-al-pico-urbion-picacho-del-camperon-y-penas-blancas-trail-running-circular-por-el-parque-natural-laguna-negra-y-circos-glaciares-de-urbion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra al Pico Urbión, Picacho del Camperón y Peñas Blancas&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking views of glacial cirques and the natural park. Many trails also provide vistas of the Urbión mountain range and the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or interesting natural monuments along the running trails?
A: Yes, the area boasts several natural attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojeda River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the region is home to significant natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of or accessible from running routes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Vinuesa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Vinuesa, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, including forest paths, mountain trails, and routes around the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo reservoir. The challenging ascents and stunning natural beauty, particularly around Laguna Negra and the glacial cirques, are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Vinuesa?
A: While many routes are challenging, families can find suitable options by looking for easier, flatter sections, especially around the reservoir. Consider routes that stick to well-maintained paths rather than steep mountain trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14939440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.5 km route that could be an option for active families.

Q: Where can I find parking near running trails in Vinuesa?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points for popular trails. For routes around Laguna Negra, there are designated parking areas. For trails starting directly from Vinuesa or Molinos de Duero, you can often find parking within the villages or near the trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo reservoir?
A: Yes, the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo reservoir is a prominent feature of the Vinuesa landscape and offers excellent running opportunities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064184945/embalse-cuerda-del-pozo-running-circular-desde-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that takes you around its scenic shores, providing beautiful water views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Vinuesa?
A: The terrain in Vinuesa is quite varied. You can expect a mix of forest paths, especially in the pine forests surrounding the villages, as well as more rugged mountain trails in the Urbión range. Routes around the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo often feature smoother, flatter sections. Elevations range from gentle slopes to significant ascents, catering to different preferences and fitness levels.
tips: If the water level is high we will not be able to see the Old Roman Bridge of Vinuesa that is hidden under the water. It is worth going when the water level allows us to appreciate such a wonder surrounded by nature.
Beautiful views over the Duero river to the town of Vinuesa from the Los Carbonales viewpoint. We can access by car, since in the same place we will find a parking lot.
